# Transcrição: Studying_other_people's_communication

- **Fonte:** Studying_other_people's_communication
- **Duração:** 01:11
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:11]

When you start to meet people who are very engaging and people who can capture your attention and build rapport with you quickly, instead of just in the moment going, oh wow, that person was really cool. I want you to study that person and start to build more awareness around, well, why was that person engaging? What did they do with their voice? How did they move their body? What were they doing with their facial expressions? Were they telling stories? Study them and pay attention to the behavioral techniques they used to be able to capture your attention, to be able to build rapport with you very quickly. And then also on the flip side, study those interactions that are not so pleasant. When you meet someone and they just rub you in the wrong way or the way they spoke, it really bothered you and you just kind of went, oh, I don't want to be around this person anymore. I want you to study those incidences as well. What made them irritating? What bothered you about that communication? How were they using their voice? How were they using their body? Was it something to do with their facial expressions? Start to build awareness on what bad communication is and what great communication is. That is a great first step. And these interactions, they happen almost on a weekly basis. People don't realize the power of communication skills. It's one of the most underrated skills ever. When you can build rapport with people quickly and you can build a connection with people quickly, it opens so many doors in life.
